content.full_text:
August 14, 1990

Sandra Taylor  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 610  
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Duties of Filing Officer

Dear Ms. Taylor:

This letter will serve to confirm our telephone conversation of this date, concerning the duties of a local Filing Officer.

The undersigned posed two situations to you. In the first, a candidate failed to file a required statement at all. In the second, two other candidates have filed statements in which, from the face of the statement, it appears each accepted excess contributions.

After discussion with you, it is our understanding that the Filing Officer has the duty, in the first situation, to notify the candidate of the failure to file and potential fine which may be assessed for continuing failure to file. In the second situation, the Filing Officer has the duty to notify the candidates of the apparent violations.

The time to comply with the notice is set at ten days from the date the notice is received by the "non-filer". You suggested a period of twenty days for the other candidates. However, the Filing Officer may select any period of time she chooses in which to require a reply.

If the candidates do not respond appropriately to the notice, the Filing Officer is to refer the matter to the enforcement agency. In our case, this agency would be the District Attorney.

If our understandings are incorrect, please do not hesitate.

Sandra Taylor  
August 14, 1990  
Page 2

to contact this office. Thank you for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

LITA O'NEILL BLATNER  
County Counsel

By [signature]  
Kathleen Bales-Lange  
Principal Attorney

cc: Mary Hopper
